pi_items.source: DXF AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard: 4 = Cyan. Verified via https://hub.qgis.org/styles/210/ and ezdxf documentation
DXF AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard: 1 = Red. Verified via https://hub.qgis.org/styles/210/ and ezdxf documentation
DXF AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard: 7 = White. Verified via https://hub.qgis.org/styles/210/ and ezdxf documentation
Task description specifies: Laser at (0,0) → Mirror1 at (300,0) → Mirror2 at (300,200) → Detector at (0,200). Verified by code execution: distance calculation confirms geometric consistency
Task description line: 'Mirror 1: 40mm line centered at (300,0), oriented to reflect beam 90 degrees UP'
Task description line: 'Mirror 2: 40mm line centered at (300,200), oriented to reflect beam 90 degrees LEFT'
Task description line: 'Lens: Ellipse centered at (150,200) (Major axis 40 vertical, Minor axis 10 horizontal)'
Calculated from beam_points: (0→300) + (300→200) + (300→200) = 300 + 200 + 300 = 800 mm. Verified by Python math.hypot() calculation

privileged_info_summary: The optical setup diagram task uses a synthetic Z-fold optical schematic with verified geometry: (1) Output saved to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/optical_setup.dxf. (2) Three layers with standard DXF colors: COMPONENTS (Cyan/4), BEAM (Red/1), LABELS (White/7). (3) Beam path connects four points—Laser(0,0)→Mirror1(300,0)→Mirror2(300,200)→Detector(0,200)—forming a Z-shape. (4) Total beam path length is 800mm (300+200+300). (5) Components include laser source and detector at (0,0) and (0,200), two mirrors at (300,0) and (300,200) oriented at 135° and 45° respectively for correct 90° reflections, and a lens at (150,200). All geometry and layer specifications are internally consistent and match the task requirements.
